summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Tournament Live Feed feature. All 14 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests verified tab switcher, photo upload button, feed display with player name/avatar/timestamp/photo, and proper access control.
passed_tests: POST /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Admin can upload photo with caption
POST /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Admin can upload photo without caption
GET /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Returns photos sorted newest first
GET /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Photos have all required fields (photo_id, tournament_id, user_id, player_name, caption, storage_path, created_at)
GET /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Private tournament requires participant status (403)
GET /api/feed/photo/{photo_id} - Endpoint is public (no auth required for img tags)
GET /api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/feed - Public tournament accessible without auth
Leaderboard page has Scores and Live Feed tab switcher
Clicking Live Feed tab shows feed with upload button and photo cards
Share a Photo button visible for admin/participants
Feed shows player name, avatar (initials), timestamp, and loaded photo
seed_data_creation: Created test tournament 'Torneo Familiar Abril' (tourn_19b19dde0591) with active status. Multiple test photos uploaded during backend testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tournament Live Feed feature fully implemented and tested. Photos are uploaded via multipart form to Emergent Object Storage, stored in MongoDB tournament_feed collection, and served via public GET /api/feed/photo/{id} endpoint. Frontend loads photos as blobs. Tab switcher on leaderboard page works correctly. Access control: private tournaments require auth + participant/admin status for feed access; public tournaments allow anonymous feed viewing. Photo serving endpoint is intentionally public to allow img tags to load.
